select_when: A team is losing clarity because too many goals, variables, or stakeholders are competing for attention at once.
Success depends on explicit boundaries around scope, acceptable trade-offs, and what the current effort will deliberately exclude.
Creative or analytical work needs a forcing function that narrows the space enough for quality decisions and movement.
The real problem is not lack of ideas but lack of boundaries strong enough to focus effort and reduce overload.
avoid_when: Old constraints are remaining unquestioned after the economics, technology, or stakeholder environment has changed.
Constraints are being written so loosely that they create false confidence in control, or so tightly that they choke off signal, adaptation, or learning.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on explicit scope, boundary-setting, and overload reduction rather than on abstract framework worship.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor prompt-engineering sprawl because the raw source is stronger on practical scope boundaries, curation, and cognitive load management.
Did not add higher-order fields because this batch stays inside the frozen Wave 1 to Wave 3 contracts.
open_questions: The typed fields compress a source that spans strategy, communication, learning, and system-boundary design.

select_when.note: The raw strengths section is explicit on focus amid competing variables, deliberate exclusions, and constraints as a forcing function.

avoid_when.note: Both anti-activation conditions are directly supported by the source danger lines on stale constraints and badly tuned constraint tightness.

input_type.note: The raw source activates on boundary and scope problems, but the compact input label is reviewed operational normalization.

output_type.note: The source yields clarified boundaries and prioritized focus; the output label compresses that into a typed artifact.

reasoning_types.note: Constraints clearly work through structure and system-boundary reasoning, but the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
